logging.c(3) Library Functions Manual logging.c(3)

NAME

logging.c - 🧪 Test utilities for logging: stdout/stderr redirection and capture helpers

SYNOPSIS

Functions


int test_logging_disable (bool disable_stdout, bool disable_stderr)
Disable stdout/stderr output for quiet test execution. int test_logging_restore (void)
Restore stdout/stderr output after test logging disable. bool test_logging_is_disabled (void)
Check if logging is currently disabled.

Detailed Description

🧪 Test utilities for logging: stdout/stderr redirection and capture helpers

Definition in file tests/logging.c.

Function Documentation

int test_logging_disable (bool disable_stdout, bool disable_stderr)

Disable stdout/stderr output for quiet test execution.

Parameters

disable_stdout If true, redirect stdout to /dev/null
disable_stderr If true, redirect stderr to /dev/null

Returns

0 on success, -1 on error

Definition at line 33 of file tests/logging.c.

33                                                                    {
34   // If already disabled, return success
35   if (logging_disabled) {
36     return 0;
37   }
38 
39   // Open /dev/null for writing
40   dev_null_fd = platform_open("/dev/null", PLATFORM_O_WRONLY);
41   if (dev_null_fd == -1) {
42     return -1;
43   }
44 
45   // Save original file descriptors if we need to redirect them
46   if (disable_stdout) {
47     original_stdout_fd = dup(STDOUT_FILENO);
48     if (original_stdout_fd == -1) {
49       close(dev_null_fd);
50       dev_null_fd = -1;
51       return -1;
52     }
53     dup2(dev_null_fd, STDOUT_FILENO);
54     // Reopen stdout to use the redirected file descriptor
55     (void)freopen("/dev/null", "w", stdout);
56     // Make stdout unbuffered to ensure immediate redirection
57     (void)setvbuf(stdout, NULL, _IONBF, 0);
58   }
59 
60   if (disable_stderr) {
61     original_stderr_fd = dup(STDERR_FILENO);
62     if (original_stderr_fd == -1) {
63       // Clean up stdout if it was redirected
64       if (disable_stdout && original_stdout_fd != -1) {
65         dup2(original_stdout_fd, STDOUT_FILENO);
66         close(original_stdout_fd);
67         original_stdout_fd = -1;
68       }
69       close(dev_null_fd);
70       dev_null_fd = -1;
71       return -1;
72     }
73     dup2(dev_null_fd, STDERR_FILENO);
74     // Reopen stderr to use the redirected file descriptor
75     (void)freopen("/dev/null", "w", stderr);
76     // Make stderr unbuffered to ensure immediate redirection
77     (void)setvbuf(stderr, NULL, _IONBF, 0);
78   }
79 
80   logging_disabled = true;
81   return 0;
82 }




References platform_open().






bool test_logging_is_disabled (void )


Check if logging is currently disabled.


Returns


true if logging is disabled, false otherwise


Definition at line 132 of file tests/logging.c.


132                                     {
133   return logging_disabled;
134 }










int
  test_logging_restore (void )


Restore stdout/stderr output after test logging disable.


Returns


0 on success, -1 on error


Definition at line 89 of file tests/logging.c.


89                                {
90   // If not disabled, return success
91   if (!logging_disabled) {
92     return 0;
93   }
94 
95   // Restore original stdout
96   if (original_stdout_fd != -1) {
97     dup2(original_stdout_fd, STDOUT_FILENO);
98     // Reopen stdout to use the restored file descriptor
99     (void)freopen("/dev/stdout", "w", stdout);
100     // Restore line buffering for stdout
101     (void)setvbuf(stdout, NULL, _IOLBF, 0);
102     close(original_stdout_fd);
103     original_stdout_fd = -1;
104   }
105 
106   // Restore original stderr
107   if (original_stderr_fd != -1) {
108     dup2(original_stderr_fd, STDERR_FILENO);
109     // Reopen stderr to use the restored file descriptor
110     (void)freopen("/dev/stderr", "w", stderr);
111     // Restore unbuffered mode for stderr
112     (void)setvbuf(stderr, NULL, _IONBF, 0);
113     close(original_stderr_fd);
114     original_stderr_fd = -1;
115   }
116 
117   // Close /dev/null file descriptor
118   if (dev_null_fd != -1) {
119     close(dev_null_fd);
120     dev_null_fd = -1;
121   }
122 
123   logging_disabled = false;
124   return 0;
125 }
